question: Do you conduct independent audits of your information security program at planned intervals?
Do you have an independent audit function that reports to senior management or the board?
Do you monitor and respond to findings from audits and assessments in a timely manner?
Do you make audit reports available to relevant stakeholders and customers upon request?
Do you track remediation of audit findings to completion?
Do you maintain certifications or attestations (SOC 2 ISO 27001 etc.) and keep them current?
Do you have a documented secure software development lifecycle (SSDLC)?
Do you perform automated security testing (SAST DAST) in your CI/CD pipeline?
Do you require security review and approval before releasing code to production?
Do you track and remediate application vulnerabilities within defined SLAs?
Do you implement runtime application protection (WAF RASP) for production applications?
Do you perform code reviews that include security considerations?
Do you maintain separation between development staging and production environments?
Do you have a documented business continuity and disaster recovery plan?
Do you test your business continuity plan at least annually?
Do you maintain redundant infrastructure to ensure availability?
Do you perform regular backups and test restoration procedures?
Do you define RTOs and RPOs for business-critical services?
Do you conduct post-incident reviews after business continuity events?
Do you have automated failover capabilities for critical systems?
Do you replicate data across geographically diverse locations?
Do you monitor system availability and alert on service degradation?
Do you include supply chain dependencies in your continuity planning?
Do you document and communicate recovery procedures to responsible personnel?
Do you have a documented change management policy?
Do you require all changes to production to go through a formal approval process?
Do you use version control for all infrastructure and application code?
Do you test changes in a non-production environment before deployment?
Do you maintain baseline configurations and detect unauthorized changes?
Do you have rollback procedures for failed changes?
Do you log all changes with who-what-when-why traceability?
Do you enforce segregation of duties between development and deployment?
Do you perform emergency change reviews after the fact when normal procedures are bypassed?
Do you have a documented encryption and key management policy?
Do you encrypt customer data at rest using AES-256 or equivalent?
Do you enforce TLS 1.2+ for all data in transit?
Do you manage encryption keys through a dedicated key management service?
Do you rotate encryption keys according to a defined schedule?
Do you maintain separation of duties for key management operations?
Do you have a process for revoking and replacing compromised keys?
Do you protect key material from unauthorized access using hardware security modules or equivalent?
Do you restrict physical access to data centers to authorized personnel only?
Do you monitor data center access with surveillance and entry logs?
Do you implement multi-layer physical access controls (badge biometric escort)?
Do you use geographically dispersed data centers for redundancy?
Do you have environmental controls (fire suppression HVAC water detection)?
Do you conduct periodic physical security assessments?
Do you maintain visitor access logs and escort procedures?
Do you have uninterruptible power supplies and backup generators?
Do you have a data classification policy that categorizes data by sensitivity?
Do you implement technical controls that enforce data handling rules based on classification?
Do you maintain an inventory of data stores containing sensitive or regulated data?
Do you implement data loss prevention controls to detect unauthorized data movement?
Do you securely delete data when it is no longer needed per retention policies?
Do you document data flows showing how customer data is processed stored and transmitted?
Do you restrict database access to authorized applications and administrators?
Do you have a data retention and disposal policy aligned with legal requirements?
Do you provide customers the ability to retrieve or delete their data on request?
Do you implement masking or tokenization for sensitive data in non-production environments?
Do you maintain data processing agreements with all sub-processors?
Do you restrict cross-border data transfers to approved jurisdictions?
Do you conduct privacy impact assessments for new data processing activities?
Do you have breach notification procedures aligned with regulatory timelines?
Do you log all access to sensitive data for audit purposes?
Do you maintain records of processing activities as required by applicable regulations?
Do you implement backup encryption for data at rest in backup storage?
Do you have a documented information security policy approved by senior management?
Do you assign clear roles and responsibilities for information security?
Do you conduct risk assessments at least annually or when significant changes occur?
Do you maintain a risk register with treatment plans for identified risks?
Do you monitor and report on security metrics to senior management?
Do you have a process for staying current with legal and regulatory obligations?
Do you document and enforce acceptable use policies?
Do you have a process for exception management when security policies cannot be followed?
Do you perform background screening for employees prior to employment?
Do you include information security responsibilities in employment contracts?
Do you provide security awareness training upon hire and annually?
Do you conduct role-specific security training for personnel in security-sensitive roles?
Do you have a disciplinary process for security policy violations?
Do you include confidentiality and non-disclosure agreements in employment terms?
Do you revoke all access and return assets upon employment termination?
Do you have a process for modifying access when employees change roles?
Do you verify that contractors and third-party personnel meet security requirements?
Do you track security training completion and send reminders for overdue training?
Do you include security responsibilities in job descriptions?
Do you perform periodic re-screening for personnel in highly privileged roles?
Do you have a process for securely managing remote workforce access?
Do you have a documented identity and access management policy?
Do you enforce unique user identification for all personnel accessing systems?
Do you implement multi-factor authentication for all user access to production?
Do you enforce strong password policies or passwordless authentication?
Do you use a centralized identity provider for single sign-on?
Do you implement role-based access control with least privilege?
Do you review and recertify user access rights at regular intervals?
Do you restrict and monitor privileged/administrative access?
Do you automate provisioning and deprovisioning tied to HR events?
Do you enforce session management controls (timeout re-auth)?
Do you log all authentication events including failures?
Do you restrict service account usage and rotate credentials?
Do you implement just-in-time or time-bound access for elevated privileges?
Do you have a process for emergency access that includes post-use review?
Do you segregate duties for critical operations to prevent single-person fraud?
Do you federate identity across cloud services rather than maintaining separate credentials?
Do you use open standards and documented APIs for data portability?
Do you provide customers with the ability to export their data in standard formats?
Do you document interoperability capabilities and limitations?
Do you support customer migration in and out of your platform?
Do you segment your network to isolate sensitive workloads?
Do you implement firewalls and network access controls at infrastructure boundaries?
Do you harden virtual machine and container images before deployment?
Do you restrict management plane access to authorized networks and personnel?
Do you maintain an inventory of all infrastructure components?
Do you monitor network traffic for anomalies and intrusion indicators?
Do you implement micro-segmentation for multi-tenant environments?
Do you scan infrastructure for misconfigurations and compliance violations?
Do you protect against distributed denial of service attacks?
Do you generate audit logs for all security-relevant events?
Do you centralize log collection from all infrastructure and applications?
Do you protect logs from tampering and unauthorized access?
Do you retain logs for a minimum period aligned with regulatory and contractual requirements?
Do you have a documented logging standard specifying what events to capture?
Do you synchronize timestamps across all systems using a reliable time source?
Do you monitor logs in near-real-time for security events?
Do you have alerting thresholds and escalation procedures for log-based detections?
Do you include identity and access events in your logging scope?
Do you correlate events across systems to detect complex attack patterns?
Do you log administrative and privileged operations separately?
Do you review logging coverage periodically to ensure no gaps?
Do you maintain audit trail integrity to support forensic investigations?
Do you have a documented security incident response plan?
Do you define roles and responsibilities for incident response team members?
Do you have detection capabilities to identify security incidents promptly?
Do you classify incidents by severity and escalate appropriately?
Do you maintain communication procedures for notifying stakeholders during incidents?
Do you conduct post-incident reviews and document lessons learned?
Do you have evidence preservation procedures for potential legal proceedings?
Do you test your incident response plan through exercises at least annually?
Do you assess the security posture of suppliers before engagement?
Do you include security requirements in supplier contracts and SLAs?
Do you maintain an inventory of all suppliers with access to data or systems?
Do you perform periodic reassessment of supplier risk?
Do you monitor supplier compliance with contractual security obligations?
Do you have procedures for responding to security incidents at suppliers?
Do you require suppliers to notify you of material security changes or breaches?
Do you assess the supply chain risk of critical software components?
Do you verify the integrity of software and updates from suppliers?
Do you have offboarding procedures for suppliers including access revocation and data return?
Do you have a documented vulnerability management policy and process?
Do you perform regular vulnerability scanning of all production systems?
Do you remediate critical vulnerabilities within defined SLAs (e.g. 72 hours)?
Do you scan for vulnerabilities in container images and cloud configurations?
Do you perform penetration testing at least annually by qualified assessors?
Do you integrate vulnerability scanning into your CI/CD pipeline?
Do you maintain threat intelligence feeds to prioritize vulnerability remediation?
Do you have a process for accepting risk on vulnerabilities that cannot be immediately remediated?
Do you track vulnerability metrics (mean time to remediate coverage etc.)?
Do you verify remediation effectiveness through re-scanning?
Do you enforce device enrollment in a centralized endpoint management solution?
Do you require full disk encryption on all corporate endpoints?
Do you deploy endpoint detection and response (EDR) on all managed devices?
Do you enforce OS and application patching within defined timeframes?
Do you restrict installation of unauthorized software on managed endpoints?
Do you enforce screen lock and automatic session timeout on endpoints?
Do you monitor endpoint compliance and quarantine non-compliant devices?
Do you support secure remote wipe for lost or stolen devices?
Do you maintain an inventory of all managed endpoints with ownership assignment?
